title: polygala vulgaris subsp. pubescens
intro: Polygala vulgaris subsp. pubescens, also known as common polygala, is a perennial plant species within the milkwort family (Polygalaceae), characterized by its hairy leaves and purple flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of polygala vulgaris subsp. pubescens include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as an immunomodulator and antioxidant. In herbal preparations, polygala vulgaris subsp. pubescens is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
